candidates.why: Primary top-line revenue label; matches Total Revenue values but is the base line rather than the explicit total.
Also represents top-line but is a total that duplicates the Revenue line; prefer the non-total label per rules.
Liability, not a revenue flow item; not appropriate for REVT.

variables.notes: Revenue is clearly the operating top-line; Total Revenue is a duplicative total line.

candidates.why: Inventory/asset component, not an income statement cost of goods sold line; using it as COGS would be incorrect.
Also appears to be an inventory/asset or supplies line on the balance sheet, not an expense line for COGS.

variables.notes: No true COGS or cost of sales line is present among candidates; provided candidates are balance sheet inventory items, not income statement expenses. A proper COGS line likely exists elsewhere but is not in the candidate list.

candidates.why: Appears to be a broad total operating cost bucket likely including direct costs/COGS; per rules we should avoid totals and cost-of-sales-like buckets for SG&A when more specific overhead components exist.
Classic SG&A / overhead component; clearly part of operating overhead and not a total. Fits XSGA_COMPONENTS definition.
Other operating income/expense; while mixed, this is closer to operating overhead than to COGS and can reasonably be included as an SG&A component when choices are limited.

variables.notes: Selected the clear overhead component (Administrative expenses) and the other operating overhead bucket (Net Other Operating Loss). Excluded Operating expenses because it is a large total likely mixing COGS and other costs, and rules say to prefer components over totals.

candidates.why: Explicit R&D expense line on the income statement; directly matches XRD definition.
Represents allocation of net income to minority shareholders, not interest expense or finance costs.
Same concept as non-controlling interests; equity attribution, not interest expense.
Balance sheet equity for minorities, not an interest expense.
Supplemental disclosure about lease-related interest/executory costs, not the main income statement interest expense line; may not align properly with XINT needs.
Interest income (positive), opposite sign of interest expense; using this for XINT would invert the concept.

variables.notes: Finance costs (interest expense) exist on the income statement but are not in the candidate list. Available candidates are either minority interest items, interest income, or a supplemental lease-related note. A human should map the true interest expense (likely the 'Finance costs' line).

candidates.why: Income from equity-method investments, not a balance sheet equity total.

candidates.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company
candidates.why: Represents book equity attributable to common shareholders; aligns with BE definition and excludes non-controlling interests, which are captured separately as MIB.
Includes both parent equity and non-controlling interests; less precise for BE when MIB is modeled separately.
A residual/balancing line, not the intended primary book equity measure.
Represents total assets/liabilities + equity; not an equity measure alone.

final_choice.row_label: Equity attributable to shareholders of the parent company

variables.notes: Chose parent-company equity to represent book equity, leaving non-controlling interests to MIB.

candidates.why: Income statement allocation of profit to minorities; useful but the balance sheet item better captures the stock of minority interest for MIB.
Duplicate concept to non-controlling interests on income statement; again a flow, not the stock.
Balance sheet minority equity, which matches the definition of MIB (minority interest / non-controlling interests) as a stock variable.

variables.notes: Used the balance sheet non-controlling interests as the minority interest stock (MIB).
